propositions: Remote visibility parity across CLI, library, and MCP facades is untested; no test proves a remote push becomes visible within the freshness window on all three.
After throttle marker expiry, each facade (CLI, library, MCP) must see a pushed remote update.
Repeated reads within the throttle window must produce exactly one fetch total via a shared throttle marker, with no double-fetch.
Each opt-out (REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off, REBAR_NO_SYNC=1, --no-pull) must keep the remote update invisible while local replay still works.
A fetch failure must yield consistent local state with no stall or corruption in each facade.
A two-clone parity test with a counted fetch seam should exercise ensure_fresh across all three facades.
